rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ALLEN J. CHUNG
rulings.Calendar Matter: PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 09/02/2020. The following issues must be addressed: 1) A receipt showing the court-approved preliminary distribution of the La Mirada property to Erica Chung as trustee of the Allen J. Chung Memorial Family Trust is not on file. The receipt must include a statement required by Probate Code § 11753(b). 2) Does Erica Chun in her individual capacity remain a "person then interested in the estate" within the meaning of § 12201(b)? If so, notice of hearing of the report must be given to her, absent a written directive signed by Erica Chung indicating that notice to her attorney is sufficient. See, e.g., LRSF 14.21C.

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: THE MITSUE GRIFFITTS 2017 LIVING TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #2, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 09/02/2020. The following issues must be addressed: 1) The beginning carry balance is different from the ending carry balance of the first account. 2) The amount of the petitioner's conservator of the estate fee reported in paragraph 6c of the petition is different from the amounts reported on Schedules C & C-1. 3) Paragraph 8 of the petition alleges that there are no outstanding liabilities against the trust estate. Who has a legal obligation to pay the Japanese translator? 4) What is the petitioner's estimate of the Japanese translator's total fees? 5) Does the $65,000 reserve include the petitioner's compensation for her trustee services after 04/01/2020? 6) Paragraph 14 of the petition and the attorney's invoice mention the "inventory and appraisal" which has never been filed. 7) Paragraph 14 incorporates Schedule H, but no such schedule is attached. 8) Schedule C-1 does not subtotal each category. CRC 7.575(c). 9) Time records supporting the $2,200 additional legal fees paid to the petitioner's attorney on 10/04/2019 must be filed. 10) Schedule A does not show any receipt of interest. Other than the CD listed on Schedule B, did the petitioner keep the trust money in interest bearing accounts during this account period? 11) Regarding the attorney's invoice: A) The attorney spent 1.0 hour on "hearing re status of filing account" on 08/06/2019, but the Court's record shows that the hearing was taken off calendar. B) Between 07/26/2019 and 07/31/2019, the paralegal spent 3.5 hours and the attorney spent 2 hours on the first account. Is the total amount for the combined services reasonable under CRC 7.703(e)(5)? C) Was it necessary for the attorney to deal with the Japanese beneficiary through the translator? Could some of the described services been delegated to the petitioner or the paralegals? D) Why did the attorney and paralegal need to spend more time on this account than the first account? 12) The $5,000 reserve must be itemized, showing an estimate of the attorney's fees for each category of post-accounting service described in paragraph 14 of the petition. ***The petitioner and her attorney need to comply with CRC 1.201 to protect personal privacy. Only the last 4 digits of financial account numbers may be disclosed in pleadings (viewable to the public in PES & PTR cases unless sealed).

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE VISHWAS NARURKAR TRUST DATED JAN-24-2019
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petitioners Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File Second Amended Petition
rulings.Rulings: The Motion for Leave to File Second Amended Petition is granted, specifically as to leave to file the Tenth Cause of Action as presented in the moving papers and counsel's supporting declaration. Petitioners are ordered to file and serve their Second Amended Petition within two days of entry of this order. While the Court is sympathetic to Respondent's frustration, granting the motion will not prejudice Respondent, and denial would deprive Petitioners of the right to assert a facially-valid cause of action. However, based on declaration of counsel, delay in bringing this motion for leave to amend was not due to Petitioners' action or inaction, but through the oversight of their attorneys. Pursuant to CCP section 473, as a condition of leave to amend, should Respondent file a motion for summary judgment to the amended pleading, Petitioners' attorneys are ordered to compensate Respondent $500 for the consequences of this belated amendment, equal to the filing fee for a motion for summary judgment. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on August 18, 2020. Remote appearance is strongly encouraged, in conformity with the current General Order of the Probate and Mental Health Division, available on the court's website at https://sfsuperiorcourt.org/divisions/probate.

rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required, primarily for the Court to extend Letters of Special Administration, if required, and to determine if the Objector should be granted additional time to retain an attorney. Jon Vigna has not yet signed (joined in) either of the "declarations" filed by counsel for the other petitioner. (The only proper pleading for providing additional or different information is, respectively, a "supplement" or an "amendment" to a petition, not an attorney declaration. [CRC 7.3.]) Please review the 6th amended general order on the court's website re the strong preference for all attorneys and parties to appear remotely as a result of the COVID-19 emergency.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF OLGA PAULINE DENEGRI
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 09/02/2020. The following issues must be addressed: 1) The notice included in the proof of publication states that the petition requests admission of the decedent's will, but according to the petition, the decedent died intestate. 2) Page 3 of the petition is missing. An amended petition including the missing page must be filed. New notice and publication are required. See CRC 7.53(a). 3) An amended petition must address the following issues: A) An appropriate box must be checked at item 3e. B) Bond waivers must be on the mandatory Judicial Council form. C) The dates of death of all deceased heirs listed at item 8 must be specified. See LRSF 14.26(E)(3). If the listed persons are issue of deceased heirs, their relationships must be specified.
